text: Statistical heterogeneity poses a significant challenge in federated learning (FL), as the resulting global model often fails to generalize effectively to the diverse local data distributions of individual clients. To address this, we introduce Federated learning with Adaptive Local Aggregation (FedALA), a novel personalized FL framework designed to selectively extract beneficial information from the global model. At the core of our approach is the Adaptive Local Aggregation (ALA) module, which adaptively merges the received global model with the existing local model based on the client's specific objective. This process occurs during local initialization, ensuring that the starting point for training is optimized for the local task in each iteration. We evaluate FedALA across five benchmark datasets in the computer vision and natural language processing domains. Experimental results demonstrate that FedALA consistently outperforms eleven state-of-the-art baselines, achieving improvements in test accuracy of up to 3.27%. Furthermore, we show that the ALA module is highly versatile; when integrated into other existing FL methods, it enhances their performance by as much as 24.19%. Our code is publicly available.
